seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in AutoGen Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in AutoGen agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AutoGen, Context Window Management issues for AI agents arise when multi-agent conversations exceed LLM token limits, causing truncated histories, forgotten context, and erratic behavior. Agents in group chats or sequential workflows accumulate verbose exchanges without truncation, leading to failed function calls and degraded performance. This is exacerbated by AutoGen's default lack of built-in token tracking across agent threads.

howItManifests.symptom: Last agent receives 'context_length_exceeded' error or garbled prompt
howItManifests.impact: Infinite conversation loops as agents repeat themselves

howItManifests.scenario: Sequential code-review workflow with nested function calls
howItManifests.symptom: Later agents produce irrelevant code due to truncated history

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-turn debate between Critic and Proposer agents
howItManifests.symptom: Token usage spikes to 100k+ without warning
howItManifests.impact: Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates

howItManifests.symptom: Agents ignore previous human corrections after 10+ turns

detection.name: Token Usage Monitor
detection.description: Tracks conversation token count per agent and alerts on 80% threshold
detection.code:
from autogen import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
import tiktoken

encoding = tiktoken.encoding_for_model('gpt-4')

def token_monitor(content, sender):
    tokens = len(encoding.encode(content))
    if tokens > 25000:  # 80% of 32k context
        return {'alert': f'High token usage: {tokens}', 'truncate': True}
    return None

# Register as reply filter
groupchat.register_reply([GroupChatManager], reply=token_monitor)

detection.name: Loop Detector
detection.description: Identifies repeated message patterns indicating context overflow loops

detection.name: Context Drift Scanner
detection.description: Compares agent responses against conversation summary to detect forgotten context

fixes.name: Dynamic Context Truncation
fixes.description: Automatically summarizes and truncates conversation history when approaching limits
fixes.code:
from autogen import register_function

turn_count = 0
context_history = []

def truncate_context(content, sender):
    global turn_count, context_history
    context_history.append(content)
    turn_count += 1
    
    if turn_count > 15 or len(''.join(context_history)) > 20000:
        # Summarize last 10 exchanges
        summary = ' '.join(context_history[-10:])[:1500]
        context_history = [f"SUMMARY: {summary}"]
        turn_count = 1
    return context_history[-1]

config_list.register_reply([UserProxyAgent], reply=truncate_context)

fixes.name: Token-Budgeted Replies
fixes.description: Enforces max tokens per response to prevent runaway conversations
fixes.code:
def token_limited_reply(messages, sender, config):
    max_tokens = 800
    prompt = messages[-1]['content']
    
    response = llm.generate(
        prompt, 
        max_tokens=max_tokens,
        temperature=0.7
    )
    
    if len(encoding.encode(response)) > max_tokens:
        response = response[:max_tokens//2] + '... [truncated]'
    
    return response

agent.register_for_llm(name="token_limited")(token_limited_reply)

fixes.name: Stateful Memory Manager
fixes.description: Compresses conversation state into key-value store instead of full history

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as a reply function filter in AutoGen workflows, automatically detecting context overflow patterns across all agent conversations. It monitors token usage in real-time, identifies loop patterns, and flags context drift without requiring manual configuration changes. Vex's agent-specific detectors catch the unique failure modes of AutoGen group chats and sequential workflows.

Once detected, Vex applies intelligent remediation: dynamic truncation, state compression, or conversation resets while preserving critical decision context. The integration requires only a single pip install and configuration line, working seamlessly with existing AutoGen agents, group chats, and human-in-loop patterns. Vex provides detailed dashboards showing token efficiency gains and failure prevention metrics specific to your AutoGen deployments.

For production AutoGen systems, Vex eliminates the need for custom monitoring by providing turn-key detection of all four common context window failure modes with automatic fixes that maintain conversation coherence.
vexAutomation.code:
pip install autogen vex-sdk

from autogen import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from vex_sdk import VexAgentMonitor

# Initialize Vex monitor for AutoGen
vex_monitor = VexAgentMonitor(
    framework='autogen',
    detectors=['context_overflow', 'loop_detection', 'token_spend', 'context_drift'],
    auto_remediate=True
)

# Single line integration
config_list = {
    'vex_filter': vex_monitor.as_reply_filter(),
    'model': 'gpt-4',
    'api_key': os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY')
}

# Your existing AutoGen workflow works unchanged
groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[coder, critic], messages=[], max_round=20)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, config_list=config_list)

manager.start()  # Vex automatically protects entire conversation
